articles.title: Book Review: ‘NB by J.C.,’ by James Campbell
articles.summary: “NB by J.C.” collects the variegated musings of James Campbell in the Times Literary Supplement.

articles.title: Book Review: ‘Dom Casmurro,’ by Machado de Assis
articles.summary: “Dom Casmurro,” by Machado de Assis, teaches us to read — and reread — with precise detail and masterly obfuscation.

articles.title: The Detective Novel ‘Whose Body?,’ by Dorothy L. Sayers, Turns 100
articles.summary: Dorothy L. Sayers dealt with emotional and financial instability by writing “Whose Body?,” the first of many to star the detective Lord Peter Wimsey.

articles.title: Book Review: ‘The Late Americans,’ by Brandon Taylor
articles.summary: Brandon Taylor’s novel circulates among Iowa City residents, some privileged, some not, but all aware that their possibilities are contracting.

articles.title: Martin Amis’s Best Books: A Guide
articles.summary: The acclaimed British novelist was also an essayist, memoirist and critic of the first rank.
